claims.text: A fall in total demand may have originated the Great Depression, but its length and severity resulted primarily from other causes.

evidence.content: By 1929, a perfect storm of unlucky factors led to the start of the worst economic downturn in U.S. history. Things were so bad that of all the days of unemployment experienced by individual American workers in American history, half occurred during the Great Depression, according to University of California, Irvine economics Professor Gary Richardson, who has done extensive research on that period and the subject of downturns in general. “For something to be as bad as the Great Depression, you really need multiple things going wrong, in the U.S. and around the world,” Richardson says. “The stock market crash did two things,” explains Mary Eschelbach Hansen, a professor of economics at American University. As Richardson notes, the U.S. economy didn’t again reach full employment until 1940—just in time for World War II to disrupt consumption with rationing needed to ensure that the military had enough resources.

additional.query: Synthesize the findings from steps 1-3 to identify a consensus among historians and economists on the main causes of the Great Depression.

Things were so bad that of all the days of unemployment experienced by individual American workers in American history, half occurred during the Great Depression, according to University of California, Irvine economics Professor Gary Richardson, who has done extensive research on that period and the subject of downturns in general. “For something to be as bad as the Great Depression, you really need multiple things going wrong, in the U.S. and around the world,” Richardson says. “The stock market crash did two things,” explains Mary Eschelbach Hansen, a professor of economics at American University. As Richardson notes, the U.S. economy didn’t again reach full employment until 1940—just in time for World War II to disrupt consumption with rationing needed to ensure that the military had enough resources.

evidence.content: The Great Depression was a devastating and prolonged economic recession that followed the crash of the United States stock market in 1929. The Great Depression: The greatest and longest economic recession in modern world history that ran between 1929 and 1941.:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/great_depression.asp-Final-8258581e56b6472fb8e1f12c3766187e.jpg). They include the stock market crash of 1929, the gold standard, a drop in lending, tariffs, banking panics, and contractionary monetary policies of the Fed. The stock market crash likely triggered the decade-long economic downturn, but most historians and economists agree that it didn't cause the Great Depression by itself. World War II created international trading channels and reversed burdensome price and wage controls, which helped the country recover from the Great Depression. The Great Depression taught economists, lawmakers, and the Fed how to better deal with the various economic circumstances and taught people how to survive in hard times. stock market chart:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/GettyImages-496030068-29ec863cca36413ab111277d250fe964.jpg). Stock Market Crash:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/Stock-Market-Crash-56a093a93df78cafdaa2dacf.jpg). A digital composite image of stock market data.:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/GettyImages-1069345714-fbda50660ce74c26944a2b03d6482473.jpg).

evidence.content: Lasting almost 10 years (from late 1929 until about 1939) and affecting nearly every country in the world, it was marked by steep declines in industrial production and in prices (deflation), mass unemployment, banking panics, and sharp increases in rates of poverty and homelessness. In the United States, where the effects of the depression were generally worst, between 1929 and 1933 industrial production fell nearly 47 percent, gross domestic product (GDP) declined by 30 percent, and unemployment reached more than 20 percent. **The gold standard.** Whatever its effects on the money supply in the United States, the gold standard unquestionably played a role in the spread of the Great Depression from the United States to other countries. Accordingly, foreign central banks attempted to counteract the trade imbalance by raising their interest rates, which had the effect of reducing output and prices and increasing unemployment in their countries.

additional.query: Search for scholarly articles and academic research papers that investigate the Great Depression, focusing on the identified causes from step 2.

evidence.content: Business Cycles and the Great Depression Depressions (or recessions) occur when there is not enough demand for all the goods and services that an economy pro-duces. The Great Depression may have originated in a fall in total demand, but its length and sever-ity resulted primarily from the unwillingness of the Federal Reserve System to prevent bank failures and to maintain a large enough money supply. The depression was particularly severe in the United States because the Federal Reserve System was obligated to follow the rules of the gold standard. Furthermore, the central banking system (in the United States, the Federal Reserve System) should create new money for the national government to borrow and spend. Foreign depositors quickly began making withdrawals from United States banks, fearing that the United States, too, would “close the gold window.” Most of the actions of the American Federal Reserve System from 1931 to 1933 can be traced to its adherence to the rules of the gold standard.

evidence.content: # Kingwood - Primary Sources. ## Great Depression Era. This is a selected bibliography of primary source collections pertaining to the Great Depression Era available online. There may also be secondary sources included in these collections and it is up to the student to evaluate if the item qualifies as a primary or secondary source. * American Life Histories: Manuscripts from the Federal Writers' Project, 1936 to 1940: Library of Congress. Transcriptions of interviews of people who participated in the Dust Bowl Migration, as well as photographs, articles and more from California State University, Bakersfield. * The Great Depression Interviews: Washington University. Videoed interviews, accompanied by transcripts, of individuals who lived through the Great Depression. * The Great Depression Primary Source Sets: Library of Congress. Primary source documents from the Library of Congress covering the Great Depression and the New Deal. Digital collection of the National Archives, covering the Great Depression and WWII. Tags: american history, history, LSC-Kingwood, Primary Sources, texas history.

additional.query: Search for historical accounts and primary sources from the time period (1929-1939) that describe the economic conditions leading up to the Great Depression.

evidence.content: The widespread prosperity of the 1920s ended abruptly with the stock market crash in October 1929 and the great economic depression that followed. At the depths of the depression, over one-quarter of the American workforce was out of work. Although the economic depression continued throughout the New Deal era, the darkest hours of despair seemed to have passed. (Totalitarian regimes in the Soviet Union and Italy predated the depression.) These regimes pushed the world ever-closer to war in the 1930s. When world war finally broke out in both Europe and Asia, the United States tried to avoid being drawn into the conflict. When Japan attacked the U.S. Naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, on December 7, 1941, the United States found itself in the war it had sought to avoid for more than two years. Mobilizing the economy for world war finally cured the depression. World War Two affected the world and the United States profoundly; it continues to influence us even today.

additional.query: Search for historical accounts and primary sources from the time period (1929-1939) that describe the economic conditions leading up to the Great Depression.

raw_payload.content: The widespread prosperity of the 1920s ended abruptly with the stock market crash in October 1929 and the great economic depression that followed. At the depths of the depression, over one-quarter of the American workforce was out of work. Although the economic depression continued throughout the New Deal era, the darkest hours of despair seemed to have passed. (Totalitarian regimes in the Soviet Union and Italy predated the depression.) These regimes pushed the world ever-closer to war in the 1930s. When world war finally broke out in both Europe and Asia, the United States tried to avoid being drawn into the conflict. When Japan attacked the U.S. Naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, on December 7, 1941, the United States found itself in the war it had sought to avoid for more than two years. Mobilizing the economy for world war finally cured the depression. World War Two affected the world and the United States profoundly; it continues to influence us even today.
raw_payload.title: Overview | Great Depression and World War II, 1929 to 1945

experiment.hypothesis: The frozen v0 ASAR pipeline can produce grounded single-goal research artifacts.
experiment.name: asar-v0-what-were-the-main-causes-of-the-great-d

goal: What were the main causes of the Great Depression?

plan.goal: What were the main causes of the Great Depression?

steps.description: Search for historical accounts and primary sources from the time period (1929-1939) that describe the economic conditions leading up to the Great Depression.
steps.expected_output: A collection of relevant articles, books, or government reports that provide insight into the causes of the Great Depression.
steps.success_criteria: Identification of at least 5 key events or factors mentioned in multiple sources as contributing to the Great Depression.

steps.description: Analyze and categorize the identified causes from step 1 into broad categories (e.g., economic, political, social).
steps.expected_output: A list of categorized causes with a brief description of each.
steps.success_criteria: Presence of at least 3 distinct categories with a minimum of 2 causes per category.

steps.description: Search for scholarly articles and academic research papers that investigate the Great Depression, focusing on the identified causes from step 2.
steps.expected_output: A set of relevant academic sources that provide in-depth analysis or empirical evidence related to the causes of the Great Depression.
steps.success_criteria: Identification of at least 3 studies that support or contradict the categorized causes from step 2.

steps.description: Synthesize the findings from steps 1-3 to identify a consensus among historians and economists on the main causes of the Great Depression.
steps.expected_output: A concise summary of the most widely accepted causes, along with any notable disagreements or debates in the field.
steps.success_criteria: Presence of a clear and well-supported conclusion that addresses the goal.

claim_verdicts.reasoning: Claim has weak lexical support from referenced evidence (downturn, economic, factors, history, led, perfect, s, start, storm, u, unlucky, worst).
Claim has weak lexical support from referenced evidence (but, demand, depression, fall, great, have, its, length, may, originated, primarily, resulted, total).
Referenced evidence did not meet the lexical support threshold. Strongest overlap was 0 informative tokens.
